header.title: How it works — full data flow

sections.title: Home page
sections.summary: File-based route at /. Uses useFetch to populate the recipe list — Nuxt handles SSR and hydration transparently.

annotations.3: useFetch dedupes the call between SSR and client hydration — no double-fetch.
annotations.8: pending and error refs come straight from the composable — no manual loading state.
annotations.14: Suspense boundary handled automatically by Nuxt at the page level.

sections.title: Recipes Nitro handler
sections.summary: Server route handling GET /api/recipes. Reads from the KV store, applies a tag filter, returns JSON.

annotations.5: getQuery — typed access to URL params, no parsing boilerplate.
annotations.12: useStorage('kv') — driver-agnostic KV; backed by Cloudflare KV in production.

sections.title: Create recipe handler
sections.summary: POST /api/recipes — validates with Valibot, writes to KV, broadcasts via the WebSocket hub.

annotations.3: readValidatedBody combines body reading with Valibot parsing in one call.
annotations.10: crypto.randomUUID() — Web Crypto API, available on both edge runtimes and Node.
annotations.16: broadcast() pushes the new recipe to every connected client over WebSockets.

sections.title: Favorites store (Pinia)
sections.summary: Pinia setup store storing favourite recipe ids in localStorage with reactive sync.

annotations.2: Setup store — composable shape; lets us return reactive refs and methods together.
annotations.6: useLocalStorage from VueUse — already debounces writes for us.
annotations.14: computed isFavorite — reactive without explicit subscription, recomputes on Set changes.
